WARNING: EVT schematic. Sequence timings and enable signal names may differ from production. Same power-on sequence as A2442 (14") — identical X2728 MLB topology.
description: Boot power-on sequence for M1 Pro/Max 16". Same Viper/Monaco VR topology as A2442. Key difference from A2681: Viper VR and Monaco VR must power up before SoC dynamic domains are ready.

sequence.name: USB-C Cable Insertion — 5V Default
sequence.action: USB-C cable inserted. Charger provides default 5V. CD3217 (UF400/UF500/UG400) internal MOSFET passes 5V.

sequence.name: Charger Input Active — PPDCIN_AON
sequence.action: Charger input path enabled. Multi-FET gate drive active. CHGR_AUX_OK asserted.

sequence.name: PPBUS_AON Established
sequence.action: Main power bus comes up from battery (12.6V) or adapter input. 99.6 Wh battery (16" specific — larger than A2442's 70 Wh).

sequence.name: PP3V8_AON Powers Up — U5700 SVR AON
sequence.action: U5700 converts PPBUS_AON to PP3V8_AON (3.8V, 30A max). Multi-phase output. Feeds both PMUs + WiFi + display + USB-C.

sequence.name: SPMU & MPMU Startup — AON and S2 Rails Cascade
sequence.action: Both PMUs receive PP3V8_AON and generate output rails in hardware-defined sequence. AON rails first, then S2, then S1.

sequence.name: Standalone VRs Power Up — 5V and 3V3
sequence.action: TPS62130 generates PP5V_S2 (5.14V, 2.1A max), separate VR generates PP3V3_S2. Load switches enabled for USB-C ports and HDMI.

sequence.name: Viper VR Powers Up — PCPU Domains
sequence.action: U9300 Viper VR controller starts multi-phase power stages (3 phases visible on page 46: PH1/PH2/PH3). PCPU clusters receive DVFS voltage. NOT present on A2681.

sequence.name: Monaco VR Powers Up — GPU/Fabric Domains
sequence.action: Monaco VR instances start (C0, C1, C2/C4). Multiple bucks per instance generate GPU, fabric, and SoC compute domains. NOT present on A2681.

sequence.name: M1 Pro/Max Hard Start Complete — SoC Commands CD3217
sequence.action: SoC completed OTP and hard start. Commands CD3217 to negotiate PD.

sequence.name: PD Negotiation → 20V Boost
sequence.action: CD3217 negotiates 20V from charger. PPVBUS transitions 5V → 20V. Protection isolation passes. PPBUS_AON stabilises.

sequence.name: Soft Start — SSD, BIOS, Display
sequence.action: SoC reads dual SSDs (SSD0 + SSD1 via Ocarina), loads firmware, display power sequencer (page 110) enables Mini-LED Liquid Retina XDR 16" panel, Luxe backlight driver activates. Dual fans spin up. Apple LOGO appears.
subsystems: Dual fans (page 66) — FAN0 left (JE500), FAN1 right (JE510)
HDMI (Madea, page 87) — if external display connected
